ConocoPhillips beat analyst expectations but saw its stock decline, while ExxonMobil achieved the
articles.content: This article analyzes the Q1 earnings performance of several diversified upstream E&P (Exploration & Production) companies, including ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP), ExxonMobil (NYSE:XOM), Devon Energy (NYSE:DVN), Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY), and Chevron (NYSE:CVX). While the sector reported strong revenues, share prices largely dropped post-earnings due to potentially higher investor expectations and shifting market narratives from AI to geopolitical risks, particularly the US' conflict with Iran. ConocoPhillips beat analyst expectations but saw its stock decline, while ExxonMobil achieved the fastest revenue growth among its peers despite a market downturn.

articles.title: SLB, Vår Energi to enhance digital field planning on NCS
articles.summary: SLB and Vår Energi are expanding their collaboration to improve well and field development planning on the Norwegian Continental Shelf (NCS) by utilizing SLB’s Delfi digital platform. This cloud-native platform integrates various aspects of exploration and production, enabling simultaneous collaboration, reducing handovers, and accelerating decision-making, ultimately aiming to shorten planning cycles from months to days. The partnership seeks to optimize resource use in mature basins and maintain production from complex offshore fields.
articles.content: SLB and Vår Energi are expanding their collaboration to improve well and field development planning on the Norwegian Continental Shelf (NCS) by utilizing SLB’s Delfi digital platform. This cloud-native platform integrates various aspects of exploration and production, enabling simultaneous collaboration, reducing handovers, and accelerating decision-making, ultimately aiming to shorten planning cycles from months to days. The partnership seeks to optimize resource use in mature basins and maintain production from complex offshore fields.

articles.title: Analysis-In China, AMD CEO Lisa Su is understated while Nvidia's Huang is more razzmatazz
articles.summary: When AMD CEO Lisa Su arrived in China last week just days after Nvidia's CEO left, she kept a much lower profile than Jensen Huang, who drew crowds in the centre of the capital as he posed for photos and ate local ‌delicacies like Beijing-style soybean paste noodles. Just a year ago at the annual Computex trade show in Taipei, Huang said Nvidia's market share in China had dropped to 50% from 95% due to U.S. export controls. Since then, that ​has effectively fallen to zero, he said this year, amid Beijing's push for self-reliance in advanced AI chips.
articles.content: When AMD CEO Lisa Su arrived in China last week just days after Nvidia's CEO left, she kept a much lower profile than Jensen Huang, who drew crowds in the centre of the capital as he posed for photos and ate local ‌delicacies like Beijing-style soybean paste noodles. Just a year ago at the annual Computex trade show in Taipei, Huang said Nvidia's market share in China had dropped to 50% from 95% due to U.S. export controls. Since then, that ​has effectively fallen to zero, he said this year, amid Beijing's push for self-reliance in advanced AI chips.

articles.summary: J. M. Smucker is employing entertainment tie-ins, such as the Milk-Bone Krypto treats for the Supergirl film, to maintain the relevance of its legacy brands and appeal to institutional investors. articles.title: Granite Construction: IIJA Ending And Margin Peaking Could Result In Downside (NYSE:GVA)
articles.summary: Granite Construction (GVA) faces potential downside as demand driven by the IIJA is expected to peak and its funding expires in September 2026. This fading tailwind, coupled with declining backlog, could lead to a reversal in revenue and margin growth post-FY27, impacting profitability. The company's recent M&A activity near the cycle peak may involve overpaying for targets, and its valuation appears stretched, with projections of a 19-26% decline as EV/EBITDA multiples normalize.
articles.content: Granite Construction (GVA) faces potential downside as demand driven by the IIJA is expected to peak and its funding expires in September 2026. This fading tailwind, coupled with declining backlog, could lead to a reversal in revenue and margin growth post-FY27, impacting profitability. The company's recent M&A activity near the cycle peak may involve overpaying for targets, and its valuation appears stretched, with projections of a 19-26% decline as EV/EBITDA multiples normalize.

articles.title: PSEi sinks to 5,700 level as MSCI reshuffle spark sell-off
articles.summary: The Philippine Stock Exchange Index (PSEi) fell 1.56 percent to 5,768.76 on Friday, reaching its weakest level since February 28 due to investor caution over the US-Iran conflict and a major MSCI index rebalancing. Trading turnover surged to P25.78 billion as foreign investors were net sellers with outflows of P6.65 billion. The Middle East conflict remains a key concern for markets due to potential impacts on global economic activity and oil prices.
articles.content: The Philippine Stock Exchange Index (PSEi) fell 1.56 percent to 5,768.76 on Friday, reaching its weakest level since February 28 due to investor caution over the US-Iran conflict and a major MSCI index rebalancing. Trading turnover surged to P25.78 billion as foreign investors were net sellers with outflows of P6.65 billion. The Middle East conflict remains a key concern for markets due to potential impacts on global economic activity and oil prices.

This comes as Home Depot reported Q1 earnings that surpassed expectations and announced a quarterly dividend of $2.33 per share. Analysts generally maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for the stock with a consensus price target of $371.39.

articles.title: DTE Energy signs 6 GWh BESS supply deal with LG for eight Michigan projects
articles.summary: DTE Energy has secured a deal with LG Energy Solution Vertech to procure 6 GWh of battery energy storage systems (BESS) for eight projects in Michigan, representing a $1.6 billion investment. This collaboration will create 1,800 jobs at LG Energy Solution's Holland plant and over 350 additional construction and operations roles. The agreement underscores DTE Energy's commitment to clean energy, grid reliability, and economic development in Michigan.

articles.title: Pittsburgh Steelers quarterback Aaron Rodgers’ estranged parents sell Chico home for $1.7 million
articles.summary: NFL star Aaron Rodgers’ parents have scored a major real estate win after offloading their longtime California home for $1.68 million just a few weeks after listing the property.
